danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile creatures (ghosts, piranhas, crabs, bulldogs, mummified mice) rendered as distinct sprites distinct from environment
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat to player character; collision results in damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Obstacles and traps visually integrated into level geometry (maze-like structures, terrain changes)
danger_signals.meaning: Non-sentient threats requiring navigation skill to avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Large-scale enemy sprite appearing at level terminus; The Glitch as final antagonist with electronic visual properties
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated danger; boss must be defeated to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual respawn mechanic; player returns to last checkpoint after defeat
danger_signals.meaning: World-state change indicating player vulnerability and resource limitation

safety_signals.visual_form: Implicit waypoints within level design where respawn occurs after death
safety_signals.meaning: Safe anchor points in level progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Beneficial items and weapon ammunition scattered throughout levels as pickable objects
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability for player empowerment and survival

safety_signals.visual_form: Commercial break sequences (Whac-A-Mole style gameplay) interspersed between boss encounters
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity for extra lives and continues; respite from primary threat cycle

safety_signals.visual_form: Game over state with option to resume from level start via password or save
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and mitigation of progress loss

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Garfield cat character sprite; comic strip origin as visual identifier
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: TV environment as player's trapped domain; levels themed as within television medium

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Electronic monster composed of spare TV parts; created from thrown components
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss entity visually distinct from regular enemies; embodies technology as corrupting force
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Television wasteland traversed during commercial breaks; electronic corruption visual theme

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Thematic enemies per level: ghosts, piranhas, crabs, bulldogs, mummified mice indicating level-specific visual identity
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Sprite variations by level theme; close-range weapons and thrown objects change per level visually
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Level-specific obstacle and hazard designs complement enemy thematic consistency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Garfield trapped inside television after falling; level environments rendered as TV-based worlds with coherent themes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Garfield navigates linearly through levels, running, jumping, climbing, swinging to progress; enemies patrol or attack on sight

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level terminus marked by boss enemy sprite; The Glitch visualized as large electronic antagonist composed of TV parts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss combat requires strategic attack and evasion; defeat triggers progression to next level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Garfield respawns at nearest checkpoint; visual reset of enemy positions and hazards; level returns to previously uncharged state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player resumes from safe position; life count decreases; game over triggered when all lives exhausted

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual transition to Television Wasteland; Garfield rockets through distinct space separate from main level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Garfield moves automatically through wasteland collecting bonus items; extra lives or continues available as rewards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game over screen presented; password or save option displayed to player
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can resume from level start via continue mechanic or reload saved progress; progression preserved

environmental_storytelling.example: Levels themed around television-trapped environments; obstacle and enemy design reflects TV-inside-world concept; each level becomes a 'channel' or broadcast world Garfield must traverse
Linear progression through levels builds journey toward confronting The Glitch as climax; commercial breaks reinforce TV broadcast metaphor
Garfield's comic strip origin informing visual identity and movement; Garfield: His 9 Lives source material influences level variety and enemy types

environmental_storytelling.technique: Hazard and obstacle layout as environmental language
environmental_storytelling.example: Maze-like level design in later stages communicates increased difficulty and exploration requirement; large level size invites multiple route discovery

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Runs, jumps, climbs, swings through levels; attacks enemies up close or throws objects; collects items and ammunition
Respawns at nearest checkpoint; returns to safe position with life counter decremented
Patrol level routes or remain stationary within designed territory
Attack on sight; require dodge/attack response from player
Boss combat encounter; requires multi-stage defeat to progress; visually embodied as electronic monster made from spare parts

notes: This game uses minimal faction-based narrative complexity; world-state communication is primarily mechanical (lives, checkpoints, continues) rather than political or faction-based. Danger and safety signals operate through game systems rather than sophisticated visual language. Level theming provides environmental storytelling but not world-state narrative complexity. The TV-inside-world conceit is primary narrative device communicated through level design and visual coherence rather than explicit faction indicators.
